hazard: Over-infusion of medication due to software loop hang
hazardous_situation: Patient receives medication at the maximum pump rate continuously, exceeding the prescribed dose, while the pump's UI fails to indicate the runaway condition.
hazardous_sequence_of_events: 1. Periodic timer ISR fails to fire due to scheduler stall. 2. Rate-control loop continues to issue motor pulses at the most-recently-commanded rate. 3. UI thread, running on a separate task, continues to display the nominal infusion-rate display. 4. Pump delivers medication at the maximum commanded rate until manually halted.
software_related_causes: Scheduler stall under heavy task load; missing independent watchdog on the rate-control loop; UI thread not gated on heartbeat from rate-control task.
harm_severity_rationale: External risk controls (clinician monitoring, infusion bag volume limit) reduce but do not eliminate the chance that an over-infusion of insulin or chemotherapy reaches a clinically significant dose before detection.
harm: Severe over-infusion with potential for life-threatening overdose (insulin shock, cytotoxicity).
risk_control_measures: REQ-PUMP-101 mandates the rate-control loop is monitored by an independent hardware watchdog that latches the motor driver into a safe state if heartbeats are missed for more than 200 ms. REQ-PUMP-102 mandates the UI thread is gated on the same heartbeat and renders an Alarm Mode banner when heartbeats stop.
demonstration_of_effectiveness: Verified by TC-PUMP-201 (functional heartbeat), TC-PUMP-202 (fault injection — scheduler stall), and TC-PUMP-203 (boundary — heartbeat exactly at 200 ms latency).
residual_risk_acceptability: Per GQP-10-02 Risk Management Report, residual risk is acceptable: the hardware watchdog and UI alarm provide redundant detection and shutoff, and the post-mitigation probability of harm is Remote with a verified detection latency under 200 ms.

requirements.text: The rate-control loop shall be monitored by an independent hardware watchdog. The motor driver shall be latched into a safe (no-pulse) state if a heartbeat from the rate-control task is missed for more than 200 ms.
The UI thread shall render an Alarm Mode banner and silence nominal-rate displays when the rate-control task heartbeat is missed for more than 200 ms.

test_cases.description: Functional verification of watchdog heartbeat reception under nominal load
test_cases.setup: Pump in standard infusion mode; rate-control task running at 50 ms cadence; hardware watchdog enabled with 200 ms latch threshold.
test_cases.steps: 1. Start a 30-minute infusion at 5 mL/hr. 2. Monitor watchdog heartbeat counter. 3. Verify motor driver remains armed and pump delivers commanded rate.
test_cases.expectedResults: 1. Heartbeat counter increments at the expected cadence. 2. Motor driver remains armed throughout. 3. Delivered volume matches commanded volume within tolerance.

test_cases.description: Fault injection — simulate scheduler stall and verify watchdog latches the motor driver
test_cases.setup: Pump in standard infusion mode; instrumented build with a debug hook to suspend the rate-control task indefinitely.
test_cases.steps: 1. Start an infusion at 5 mL/hr. 2. Trigger the debug hook to suspend the rate-control task. 3. Observe the watchdog timer and motor driver state. 4. Observe the UI banner.
test_cases.expectedResults: 1. Watchdog counter stalls as expected. 2. After 200 ms, the motor driver latches to the safe state and stops issuing pulses. 3. The UI renders the Alarm Mode banner and silences the nominal-rate display.

test_cases.description: Boundary — heartbeat latency exactly at 200 ms threshold
test_cases.setup: Pump in standard infusion mode; instrumented build with a debug hook to delay the rate-control task heartbeat by a configurable amount.
test_cases.steps: 1. Start an infusion. 2. Configure the debug hook to delay heartbeats by 199 ms; verify motor driver remains armed. 3. Configure the debug hook to delay heartbeats by 200 ms; verify motor driver remains armed at the boundary. 4. Configure the debug hook to delay heartbeats by 201 ms; verify motor driver latches to the safe state.
test_cases.expectedResults: 1. At 199 ms delay the motor driver is armed. 2. At 200 ms delay the motor driver is armed (inclusive boundary). 3. At 201 ms delay the motor driver latches to the safe state and the UI Alarm Mode banner renders.

design_docs.name: Rate Control Loop and Watchdog Architecture
design_docs.description: Describes the segregation of the rate-control task from the UI task, the heartbeat protocol, the hardware watchdog wiring, and the safe-state latch behavior of the motor driver.
